name: Tour of Lake Matemale
description: Cycle an easy 9.3 miles (15.0 km) around Lake Matemale, enjoying scenic Pyrenees views and gaining 706 feet (215 metres).
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Forêt de la Matte are situated on the Capcir plateau at approximately 1500 meters elevation, characterized by gentle slopes. The region features ancient Scots pines, some dating back to the 17th century, creating an expansive forested environment. Many routes border the artificial Lac de Matemale, offering views of the water and the Pic de Caputxet. This area is recognized as a protected Zone Naturelle d'Intérêt Écologique, Faunistique et Floristique (ZNIEFF). Best easy hiking trails around Forêt de la Matte The most popular easy hiking route is Les Angles Old Town – Lake Trail loop from Les Angles, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This route offers a varied landscape connecting the old town with the lake. Another top favourite among local hikers is Lake Matemale – Lake Matemale loop from Matemale, an easy 3.4 miles (5.5 km) path. This trail circumnavigates Lac de Matemale, providing continuous views of the water and surrounding forest. Local hikers also love the Forêt de la Matte loop from Matemale, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through ancient Scots pine forest, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Hiking around Forêt de la Matte is defined by ancient Scots pine forests, the tranquil Lac de Matemale, and gentle, accessible terrain. The network offers options for various ability levels, with a focus on easy hiking trails. The routes in Forêt de la Matte are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,200 reviews. More than 13,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Forêt de la Matte's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Forêt de la Matte?
A: Forêt de la Matte offers a good selection of easy hiking trails. There are &lt;strong&gt;13 easy routes&lt;/strong&gt; out of a total of 19 trails in the area, making it an ideal destination for relaxed walks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hikes in Forêt de la Matte?
A: The easy hikes in Forêt de la Matte are characterized by &lt;em&gt;gentle slopes&lt;/em&gt; and generally accessible terrain. The forest is situated on the Capcir plateau at an elevation of approximately 1500 meters, providing a serene environment primarily composed of ancient Scots pines.

Q: Are there any circular easy hiking routes in Forêt de la Matte?
A: Yes, several easy circular routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24685748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Matemale – Lake Matemale loop from Matemale&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.4 miles (5.5 km) and circumnavigates the tranquil Lac de Matemale. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6984138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forêt de la Matte loop from Matemale&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail through the ancient Scots pine forest.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see on an easy hike here?
A: Many easy trails offer picturesque views of the artificial &lt;strong&gt;Lac de Matemale&lt;/strong&gt;, often framed by the surrounding woodlands and with the Pic de Caputxet in the background. You'll also experience the majestic, centuries-old Scots pines, some dating back to the 17th century, which create a unique forested environment. The area is also a protected Zone Naturelle d'Intérêt Écologique, Faunistique et Floristique (ZNIEFF).

Q: Are the easy hiking trails in Forêt de la Matte suitable for families?
A: Absolutely. The gentle slopes and well-developed network of trails make Forêt de la Matte very family-friendly. Many paths are suitable for children, and the area around Lac de Matemale offers additional activities like pedal boating and adventure courses, making it a comprehensive destination for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the easy hiking trails?
A: Yes, many trails in Forêt de la Matte are dog-friendly. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas like the ZNIEFF, to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Always check local signage for specific rules.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the easy trails in Forêt de la Matte?
A: The easy trails in Forêt de la Matte are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.5 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 1,200 reviews. Reviewers often praise the serene forested environment, the beautiful views of Lac de Matemale, and the accessibility of the paths, making them perfect for a relaxing outdoor experience.

Q: Is there parking available near the easy hiking trails?
A: Yes, there are designated parking areas conveniently located near trailheads, particularly around Lac de Matemale and the villages of Matemale and Les Angles. This makes accessing the easy hiking routes straightforward for visitors arriving by car.

Q: Are there any easy trails that connect to nearby towns or villages?
A: Yes, some easy trails offer connections to local villages. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40059142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Angles Old Town – Lake Trail loop from Les Angles&lt;/a&gt; is a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) route that connects the charming old town with the lake, offering a varied landscape and easy access to amenities.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike the easy trails in Forêt de la Matte?
A: Forêt de la Matte is beautiful year-round, but the best time for easy hiking is typically from &lt;strong&gt;late spring through early autumn&lt;/strong&gt; (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally mild, and the trails are clear. The Scots pine forest provides ample shade during warmer days, and the autumn colors are particularly stunning.

Q: Are there picnic areas or facilities along the easy trails?
A: Yes, for your convenience, the area is equipped with &lt;strong&gt;picnic tables and designated areas for grilling&lt;/strong&gt;. These facilities are often found near Lac de Matemale and at various points along the more popular trails, enhancing the outdoor experience for hikers.

Q: Can I find refreshments or accommodation near the easy hiking trails?
A: Yes, the nearby villages of Matemale and Les Angles offer various options for refreshments, including cafes and restaurants. You can also find accommodation in these areas, ranging from hotels to guesthouses, providing convenient bases for exploring the easy hiking trails of Forêt de la Matte.
tips: Very large lake (retained on the Aude) with a beautiful view of the two roads that go down there. The one that goes to Angles seemed to me more pleasant in terms of curve, and circulation.

faqs.question: How long does it take to cycle the Tour of Lake Matemale?
faqs.answer: This specific route, designed for racebikes, typically takes around 45 minutes to complete. However, the area around Lake Matemale offers various cycling routes, with longer options that can take several hours depending on the distance and elevation.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of Lake Matemale suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this particular Tour of Lake Matemale route is classified as 'easy' and is well-suited for beginners and families. It's a shorter loop with minimal elevation gain, primarily on paved surfaces, making it accessible for all skill levels.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling around Lake Matemale?
faqs.answer: The terrain for this specific route is mostly paved and relatively flat, contributing to its 'easy' difficulty rating. Generally, cycling routes around Lake Matemale can vary, with some longer options including more significant elevation changes and occasionally unpaved segments, but this tour is designed for road cycling on smooth surfaces.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Tour of Lake Matemale?
faqs.answer: The route offers scenic views of the artificial Lake Matemale itself, situated at an altitude of 1,541 meters. You'll also enjoy picturesque backdrops of the Pyrenees mountains, including the Carlit Massif, and pass through forests within the natural park.

faqs.question: Where can I park to cycle the Tour of Lake Matemale?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available at various points around Lake Matemale, especially near popular access points and villages like Matemale or Les Angles. Look for designated parking areas close to the lake shore or trailheads to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Tour of Lake Matemale?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically from late spring through early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, offering ideal conditions for enjoying the scenic views without the challenges of winter snow or extreme heat.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of Lake Matemale dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is suitable for cycling, specific regulations for dogs may apply in the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées catalanes. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural park areas, and to ensure they are comfortable with cycling speeds if accompanying you.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées catalanes?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to access and cycle within the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées catalanes. However, always respect local regulations, especially regarding protected areas, wildlife, and private property.

faqs.question: Does the Tour of Lake Matemale intersect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with sections of several other paths. You'll find it overlaps with parts of El Camí IT1TR4 El Camí Capçaler: de Salzes a Balaguer, Chemin Vauban, and Tour du Capcir, offering opportunities to explore further if you wish.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or facilities available along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, given the popularity of Lake Matemale, you'll find cafes, restaurants, and other facilities in the nearby villages and around the lake itself. These provide convenient stops for refreshments or a meal during or after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for cycling this route?
faqs.answer: For this easy racebike tour, bring your bicycle, a helmet, water, and appropriate cycling attire. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended, especially during sunny days. A basic repair kit can be useful, though the route is short and accessible.
